summary: PR 2597 made ftCo_DamageFall.c a Matching object in configure.py and applied a narrow source change in ftCo_80090594: copying the FtMotionId parameter msid into a local FtMotionId motion_id before passing it to calcShift. No PR body, review comments, or issue comments were present in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Changed ftCo_DamageFall.c from NonMatching to Matching in the MatchingFor object list.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk at MatchingFor list

key_files.role: Adjusted ftCo_80090594 by introducing a local FtMotionId motion_id initialized from msid and using it in the calcShift call.

what_changed.text: Marked melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageFall.c as Matching instead of NonMatching in configure.py.
In ftCo_80090594, added a typed local variable `FtMotionId motion_id = msid;`.
Changed the calcShift call to pass `motion_id` instead of the original parameter `msid`.

smart_moves.text: Kept the matching-oriented source edit very narrow: one local alias in one function plus the configure.py matching status flip.
Used the existing typed enum-like motion ID type for the local copy rather than an untyped temporary.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A small typed local copy of a parameter can be part of closing a function/object match, but the PR slice only supports this as a local tactic for ftCo_80090594, not as a broad standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local FtMotionId motion_id introduced and used in calcShift

raw_diff: configure.py changed ftCo_DamageFall.c to Matching

naming_conventions.text: The added local uses a clear role-preserving name `motion_id` for the existing `FtMotionId msid` parameter.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce a typed local alias for a parameter before a call to influence local lifetime/register/codegen while preserving source meaning.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No objdiff, build log, or reviewer explanation is included in the slice; the match is inferred from the PR title and configure.py Matching change.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: After the source adjustment, link the object by changing its configure.py status from NonMatching to Matching.

Treat this as a localized matching tactic with no broader standard unless corroborated by more PRs or verifier evidence.

rejection_notes.text: Do not promote `always copy motion ID parameters to motion_id before calls` as a standard; this PR only shows one local source edit and lacks verifier or author explanation.
